task_title: Narrative: what this support ticket is actually about

scores.justification: Agent successfully fetched ticket t019 using `./cli/bin/ctl get tickets t019 --format json` (message[4]) and also fetched the owning user u042's record using `./cli/bin/ctl get users u042 --format json` (message[6]).

Agent explicitly connected the billing issue to u042's suspension on 2026-04-22 at 11:47:00Z, as shown in the user record (message[6]) and referenced in the final paragraph stating 'account suspension on the morning of April 22 at 11:47 UTC'.

Agent fetched all three flagged orders (o102, o107, o115) in messages[7-9] and confirmed their flag_reason as 'billing anomaly; see ticket t019', explicitly connecting them in the final paragraph: 'flagged all three orders and then automatically escalated to a full account suspension'.

The paragraph offers a plausible root-cause hypothesis: 'an automated fraud-prevention pipeline interpreted her legitimate cross-currency purchasing pattern as suspicious activity and applied a blunt enforcement action — suspension — rather than a softer hold or manual review', which aligns with automated fraud detection misfiring.

The paragraph invents specific currency amounts (£852.34, $862.14, €1,648.12) that do not appear in the transcript; the actual amounts are in cents (85234 GBP, 86214 USD, 164812 EUR) and the agent converted them without evidence from the data.
